title: Vowels Part 2: Long Sounds
description: Now let's learn the LONG vowels (दीर्घ स्वर) of Hindi! These are stretched-out versions of sounds you already know, plus some new ones. Long vowels are held for roughly twice the duration of short vowels. Getting the length right is crucial — 'din' (दिन = day) has a short इ, but 'deen' (दीन = poor) has a long ई. Different vowel, different meaning!
keyPoints: Long vowels are held for about twice the duration of short vowels
7 long vowels: आ (aa), ई (ee), ऊ (oo), ए (e), ऐ (ai), ओ (o), औ (au)
Each short vowel has a long counterpart: अ→आ, इ→ई, उ→ऊ
Vowel length changes meaning: दिन (day) vs दीन (poor)

vietnamese: aa — nguyên âm dài 'a' như trong 'father'
ee — nguyên âm dài 'ee' như trong 'see'
oo — nguyên âm dài 'oo' như trong 'boot'

hint: This is अ with an extra vertical stroke on the right. Draw अ first, then add the line.
Similar to इ but with longer curves. Draw the body and add the headline on top.

english: e — as in 'they' or 'play' (no short equivalent!)
ai — as in 'aisle' or like 'a' in 'apple'

text: 'boot' के 'oo' जैसी ध्वनि कौन सा स्वर है?
subtext: Think of a long, rounded 'oo' sound

hint: Similar to उ but with an additional curve. Make the tail longer and more pronounced.

title: Long Vowels — Key Takeaways
takeaways: ऐ (ai) — 'aisle', a wider version of ए
औ (au) — 'cow', a wider version of ओ
Long vowels are held about twice as long as short vowels
Key words: आम (mango), ईख (sugarcane), ऊन (wool), एक (one), ऐनक (glasses), ओस (dew), औरत (woman)
